item.title: Elder Cūḷaka: Peacocks call, beautiful-crested, with lovely tail feathers, Their necks bright blue, beaks beautiful, voices melodious. This land is well covered with grass, well…
item.description:
Elder Cūḷaka:

Peacocks call, beautiful-crested, with lovely tail feathers,
Their necks bright blue, beaks beautiful, voices melodious.
This land is well covered with grass, well moistened with water,
Well covered by the sky, well covered by rain clouds.

The human body is well healthy, the mind good, practicing jhāna.
Excellent is the well-arisen mind in the Buddha's well-spoken teaching.
Experience the path leading to the Deathless,
That path is unsurpassed, the pure, healthy,
Subtle, fine path, well seen.

From: Theragāthā 2.46
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Poṭṭhapāda, there are three kinds of self-identification: the gross self-identification, the mind-made self-identification, and the formless self-identification. Poṭṭhapāda, what is the gross self-identification? Having…
item.description:
- Poṭṭhapāda, there are three kinds of self-identification: the gross self-identification, the mind-made self-identification, and the formless self-identification. Poṭṭhapāda, what is the gross self-identification? Having form, composed of the four great elements, nourished by solid food — this is the gross self-identification. What is the mind-made self-identification? Having form, mind-made, complete with all major and minor parts, with all faculties intact — this is the mind-made self-identification. What is the formless self-identification? Formless, made of perception — this is the formless self-identification.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 9 – Poṭṭhapādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Thus, Ānanda, action is the field, consciousness is the seed, craving is the moisture. For beings obstructed by ignorance and fettered by craving, consciousness is…
item.description:
Thus, Ānanda, action is the field, consciousness is the seed, craving is the moisture. For beings obstructed by ignorance and fettered by craving, consciousness is established in a lower realm. Thus there is the production of renewed existence in the future. Thus, Ānanda, existence comes to be.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.76
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice…
item.description:
A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice of superior conduct. Having fulfilled the practice of superior conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill the learner's practice. Having fulfilled the learner's practice, it is possible that he will fulfill the rules of virtuous conduct. Having fulfilled the rules of virtuous conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill right view. Having fulfilled right view, it is possible that he will fulfill right concentration.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 5.21
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Kātiyāna: O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up! Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself! O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy — Do not…
item.description:
Elder Kātiyāna:

O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up!
Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself!
O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy —
Do not let Death deceive and conquer you.

Like waves covering the great ocean,
So birth and old age cover and bury you.
Make yourself a safe island,
For no one else can serve and help you as a refuge.

The Teacher has laid down the path crossing beyond bonds,
Crossing beyond the fear of birth and old age.
In the evening and in the morning, live heedfully,
Attentive, persisting, in devoted effort.

Abandoning former bonds, wearing the outer robe,
Head shaven smooth, eating almsfood,
Do not delight in playing, do not indulge in sleeping.
Strive in meditation, O Kātiyāna!

Meditate, be victorious, O Kātiyāna!
Be skilled in the path, secure from bonds.
Attain supreme purity, you will attain Nibbāna,
Like water extinguishing fire.

Light is produced, the radiance is still weak,
Like a reed bent down by the wind.
O relative of Suda, do not grasp thus!
Expel Māra, dispassionate toward all feelings,
Wait for your time! Here you will be cooled.

From: Theragāthā 6.7
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of…
item.description:
Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. For, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. Therefore, a disciple, having undertaken and trained in the training rules, should have unimpaired conduct, uncut conduct, unstained conduct, unblemished conduct in that training. Thus, bhikkhus, is the benefit of training.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.245
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Then the friends of the good man Raṭṭhapāla went to his parents and said: - Dear mother and father, the good man Raṭṭhapāla is lying…
item.description:
Then the friends of the good man Raṭṭhapāla went to his parents and said:
- Dear mother and father, the good man Raṭṭhapāla is lying on the bare ground and says: "Here I will die, or else I will go forth." If you do not give your permission for the good man Raṭṭhapāla to go forth from home to homelessness, he will die right there. But if you give your permission for him to go forth, you will be able to see him after he has gone forth. If the good man Raṭṭhapāla does not find delight in going forth, he will have no other way but to return here. Please give your permission for him to go forth.
- Dear children, we give our permission for the good man Raṭṭhapāla to go forth from home to homelessness, but after going forth, he must visit his parents.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 82 – Raṭṭhapālasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Bhikkhus, the arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the eye is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death.…
item.description:
- Bhikkhus, the arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the eye is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death. The arising, continuation, production, manifestation of the ear ... of the nose ... of the tongue ... of the body ... of the mind is the arising of suffering, the continuation of disease, the manifestation of aging and death. The cessation, stilling, passing away of the eye is the cessation of suffering, the stilling of disease, the passing away of aging and death. The cessation, stilling, passing away of the ear ... nose ... tongue ... body ... mind is the cessation of suffering, the stilling of disease, the passing away of aging and death.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 26.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: ... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One: A father delights in his son, A cow-owner…
item.description:
... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
A father delights in his son,
A cow-owner delights in his cows,
A person with attachments delights,
One without attachments does not delight.
( Blessed One ):
A father grieves over his son,
A cow-owner grieves over his cows,
A person with attachments grieves,
One without attachments does not grieve.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 1.12
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, brahmin, a person is not dispassionate towards sensual pleasures, not free from desire, not free from affection, not free from thirst, not free from…
item.description:
Here, brahmin, a person is not dispassionate towards sensual pleasures, not free from desire, not free from affection, not free from thirst, not free from fever, not free from craving. Then a severe illness arises in him. Affected by that severe illness, he reflects: 'Indeed, these delightful sensual pleasures will leave me,' or 'I will leave these delightful sensual pleasures.' He grieves, laments, weeps, beats his breast, and falls into confusion. This, brahmin, is a person who, by nature, being subject to death, is afraid and falls into terror when thinking of death.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.184
📖 Read this sutta
